blurb: A neat symmetrical rosette that blushes pink-red at the leaf tips in good light. The classic 'hen' succulent — forgiving, photogenic, and generous with offsets.

care.light: The more bright light (including direct sun) it gets, the tighter the rosette and the deeper the tip colour. In low light it pales, stretches, and the rosette opens up.
care.water: Soak-and-dry, watering the soil and never letting water sit in the crown — trapped water rots the centre. Wait until the lower leaves feel slightly soft.
care.soil: A gritty, fast-draining mix keeps the roots healthy and the rosette compact.
care.temperature: Room temperature is ideal; protect from frost — bring it in before the first hard cold.
care.dormancy: A warm-season grower that slows in deep winter; keep it bright and on the dry side then.
care.feeding: A dilute feed once or twice through spring and summer.
care.potting: Repot every year or two in a pot with drainage; a snug pot and gritty mix keep it stress-coloured.
care.propagation: Leaves laid on dry mix, offsets pulled from the base, or beheading a stretched rosette and re-rooting the top.
care.bloom: Bell-shaped flowers on an arching one-sided stalk in the warmer months.
care.troubles: Rot from an overwatered crown, stretching (etiolation) in low light, and mealybugs tucked between leaves.
